hero_headline: ChatGPT Grows Up, and Schools Have to Keep Pace
intro: ChatGPT has gone from novelty to fixture, and this issue looks at what that means for us. We have a fresh K-12 offering built for teachers, a few features worth your attention, and a $4 billion bet from Microsoft on AI in education. We also keep it honest with the harder stuff, cheating and the legal questions that follow when AI shows up in a discipline file.

items.title: Education in the age of ChatGPT
items.takeaway: A grounded look at how ChatGPT is reshaping the basics of teaching, learning, and what we expect students to do on their own.

items.title: Putting AI to Work in Schools Is Difficult. A New Toolkit Outlines How to Do It
items.takeaway: Moving from pilots to real classroom use is the hard part, and this new toolkit gives leaders a practical path instead of vague enthusiasm.

items.title: Microsoft Pledges $4 Billion Toward A.I. Education
items.takeaway: When a company puts $4 billion behind AI training and tools for schools, it tells you where the money and the expectations are heading.

items.title: AI in the classroom: How it's impacting teachers and students
items.takeaway: A clear, on-the-ground view of how AI is already changing daily work for teachers and students, not in theory but right now.

items.title: ChatGPT for K-12 teachers
items.takeaway: OpenAI built a version aimed squarely at K-12 teachers, worth a look if you want lesson planning and feedback help without piecing it together yourself.

items.title: 3 ChatGPT Features Worthy of a Teacher's Attention
items.takeaway: Three concrete features you can use this week, skip the hype and grab the parts that actually save time.

sections.name: Proceed With Caution
sections.note: A standing spot for the honest watch-outs. AI is a tool, not a shortcut around judgment.

items.title: Schools scramble to police AI cheating
items.takeaway: Detection tools are unreliable and students are ahead of the curve, so the real answer is rethinking assignments, not chasing a perfect catcher.

items.title: Did This Parent Hire a Lawyer? Schools Face a New Challenge With AI
items.takeaway: When an AI accusation lands in a student's record, parents are pushing back hard, a reminder to keep due process tight before you act.

editor_pick.title: ChatGPT for K-12 teachers
editor_pick.blurb: If you only click one link this month, make it this one. It is built for K-12 teachers and gives you a real starting point for lesson planning and feedback instead of a blank chat box.
